edges.description: Transformed HRS cells remodel a supportive inflammatory niche.
edges.source: Germinal Center B-Cell Origin of HRS Cells

edges.description: JAK/STAT signaling supports inflammatory niche formation.
NF-kappaB signaling sustains the inflammatory tumor niche.
Constitutive NF-kappaB signaling cooperates with checkpoint-ligand upregulation to promote immune escape.
EBV-driven NF-kappaB activation reinforces the inflammatory tumor niche.
The inflammatory niche is coupled to ineffective anti-tumor immunity.
The HRS-supported inflammatory niche contributes to bulky nodal enlargement.
Cytokine-rich tumor inflammation contributes to B symptoms including fever.
Cytokine-rich tumor inflammation contributes to B symptoms including night sweats.
Cytokine-rich tumor inflammation contributes to systemic B symptoms including weight loss.

nodes.description: HRS cells persist within a dense inflammatory infiltrate that they actively recruit and shape through cytokine and chemokine signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma - Old Disease, New Directions: An Update on Pathology, Molecular Features and Biological Prognostic Markers.
evidence.snippet: HRS cells are scattered within a dense inflammatory infiltrate, and through a network of cytokines and chemokines they shape their microenvironment, evade immune response, survive, and grow.

nodes.description: In Epstein-Barr virus-positive classic Hodgkin lymphoma, viral latent membrane protein 1 mimics active CD40 signaling and drives NF-kappaB activation in Hodgkin/Reed-Sternberg cells.

evidence.snippet: In Epstein-Barr virus-positive cases of classical HL, the virus-encoded latent membrane protein 1 causes NF-κB activation by mimicking an active CD40 receptor.

nodes.description: Fever is a classical B symptom that may accompany nodal disease.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma: A Nonclassic Presentation.
evidence.snippet: Hodgkin lymphoma classically presents as lymphadenopathy with contiguous spread with or without associated B symptoms including night sweats, weight loss, and fever.

nodes.description: The malignant Hodgkin/Reed-Sternberg cell is derived from germinal center B cells but loses many typical B-cell features during transformation.
nodes.id: Germinal Center B-Cell Origin of HRS Cells

evidence.reference_title: Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma - Old Disease, New Directions: An Update on Pathology, Molecular Features and Biological Prognostic Markers.
evidence.snippet: The hallmark of this disease is the neoplastic Hodgkin/Reed-Sternberg (HRS) cell, which is favored to be derived from germinal center B-cells but has lost many of the B-cell markers.

nodes.description: Classic Hodgkin lymphoma usually presents with nodal enlargement and contiguous nodal spread.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma: A Nonclassic Presentation.
evidence.snippet: Hodgkin lymphoma classically presents as lymphadenopathy with contiguous spread with or without associated B symptoms including night sweats, weight loss, and fever.

nodes.description: Recurrent mutations in classic Hodgkin lymphoma activate JAK/STAT signaling programs that support tumor-cell survival and cytokine-responsive growth.

biological_processes: cell surface receptor signaling pathway via JAK-STAT

evidence.reference_title: Analysis of the mutational landscape of classic Hodgkin lymphoma identifies disease heterogeneity and potential therapeutic targets.
evidence.snippet: However, several variants were consistently detected in genes related to relevant signaling pathways, such as GM-CSF/IL-3, CBP/EP300, JAK/STAT, NF-kappaB, and numerous variants of genes affecting the B-cell receptor (BCR) pathway, such as BTK, CARD11, BCL10, among others.

nodes.description: Recurrent lesions affecting negative regulators and upstream activators of NF-kappaB signaling drive constitutive survival signaling in Hodgkin/Reed-Sternberg cells.

evidence.snippet: This includes interaction with other cells in the lymphoma microenvironment through CD30, CD40, BCMA and other receptors, but also recurrent somatic genetic lesions in various factors of the NF-κB pathway, including destructive mutations in negative regulators of NF-κB signaling (e.g. TNFAIP3, NFKBIA), and copy number gains of genes encoding positive regulators (e.g. REL, MAP3K14).

nodes.description: Night sweats are part of the classical B-symptom complex at presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma: A Nonclassic Presentation.
evidence.snippet: Hodgkin lymphoma classically presents as lymphadenopathy with contiguous spread with or without associated B symptoms including night sweats, weight loss, and fever.

nodes.description: PD-1 blockade with nivolumab is an established treatment option in previously treated classic Hodgkin lymphoma and is tightly aligned to the disease's 9p24.1-driven checkpoint biology.

evidence.snippet: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the efficacy and safety of Nivolumab in previously treated (cohorts, A, B & C) or newly diagnosed (cohort D) classical Hodgkin Lymphoma participants.

nodes.description: Near-universal 9p24.1 copy gain increases PD-1 ligand expression on HRS cells and contributes to immune escape in classic Hodgkin lymphoma.

biological_processes: Negative Regulation of T Cell Mediated Immunity

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analyses of flow-sorted Hodgkin Reed-Sternberg cells reveal complementary mechanisms of immune evasion.
evidence.snippet: HRS cells exhibit near-universal somatic copy gains of chromosome 9p/9p24.1, which increase expression of the programmed cell death protein 1 (PD-1) ligands.

nodes.description: PD-1 blockade with pembrolizumab is active in relapsed or refractory classic Hodgkin lymphoma.

evidence.reference_title: Phase II Study of the Efficacy and Safety of Pembrolizumab for Relapsed/Refractory Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: Pembrolizumab was associated with high response rates and an acceptable safety profile in patients with rrHL, offering a new treatment paradigm for this disease.

nodes.description: Unintentional weight loss is a classical B symptom of active disease.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma: A Nonclassic Presentation.
evidence.snippet: Hodgkin lymphoma classically presents as lymphadenopathy with contiguous spread with or without associated B symptoms including night sweats, weight loss, and fever.
